claim: YOLO provider dispatch API/state handling has recent changes in evidence-dispatch.ts and provider-adapter.ts to support demand evidence mode with boundary enforcement and provider-specific configuration isolation. Cross-provider model leakage prevention exists but requires stress testing with real agents.

evidence.summary: settingsValue function added to handle JSON settings strings vs file paths for Claude provider configuration
evidence.why: New capability for inline settings in evidence mode

evidence.summary: Claude provider now supports claude_tools, claude_allowed_tools, claude_disallowed_tools, claude_disable_slash_commands, claude_no_session_persistence, and max_budget_usd flags
evidence.why: Tool profile management for evidence mode boundary enforcement

evidence.summary: Codex provider includes explicit Claude model leak prevention: if model is empty and ai.model contains claude, ai.model is cleared to avoid cross-provider model confusion
buildBoundarySnapshot function creates file digests before and after agent execution to detect mutations, using git ls-files when available or walkFiles fallback
diffBoundarySnapshots compares before/after snapshots to detect added, modified, or deleted files for boundary violation detection
evidence.why: Mutation detection logic for boundary enforcement

evidence.summary: executionConfig function normalizes provider settings including agent_tool_profile mapping to research/full/boundary for Claude, and sets claude_permission_mode to bypassPermissions
evidence.why: Provider-specific configuration normalization for evidence mode

evidence.summary: runDemandEvidenceDispatchRuntime spawns agents, captures results, audits boundary changes, and updates readiness with blockers for mutations or missing external evidence
evidence.why: Complete evidence dispatch lifecycle with boundary enforcement

evidence.summary: Test codex dispatch does not inherit claude default model or force read-only sandbox, verifying provider isolation
Test claude dispatch keeps default tools available and relies on boundary enforcement instead of tool restriction
evidence.why: Boundary enforcement over tool restriction approach validation

evidence.summary: Test boundary blocks project file mutation even when agent results pass, verifying boundary enforcement actually works
Claude provider demand dispatch does not use allowedTools/disallowedTools/tools restrictions; safety comes from prompt contract, artifact root, boundary snapshots, and readiness blockers
evidence.why: Design rationale for boundary-based safety vs tool restrictions

evidence.summary: demandRequestsExternalResearch and externalEvidencePresent functions detect when demand requires external research and verify scope=external evidence exists
evidence.why: External evidence validation for demand compliance

evidence.summary: buildDemandEvidenceAgentPrompt generates protocol-specific prompts with boundary rules, scope requirements, and external research instructions based on tool profile
evidence.why: Agent prompt generation with boundary and scope contract enforcement

evidence.summary: Recent dispatch execution shows clean boundary with project_mutation: clean and zero changes, proving boundary enforcement works in practice
evidence.why: Real-world evidence that boundary enforcement is functioning

evidence.summary: Provider runs show successful Claude provider execution with json_repaired: false for all three agent roles, indicating stable JSON parsing
evidence.why: Evidence of stable provider execution in evidence mode

assumptions: The git working tree with 55 modified files includes intentional changes for evidence dispatch and is not a symptom of provider configuration drift
Cross-provider model leakage prevention in provider-adapter.ts line 522-523 is sufficient but may not cover all edge cases
Stress test artifacts in .yolo/stress/ indicate previous testing but current demand claims these tests need to be run with real Claude -p agents
risks: A provider-specific assumption in executionConfig or buildProviderInvocation could silently weaken dispatch or break real agent calls if provider isolation is incomplete
Tool profile flags (claude_tools, claude_allowed_tools, claude_disallowed_tools) may have different behavior across Claude versions or configurations
Boundary enforcement relies on file system state; concurrent modifications or timing issues could produce false negatives or positives
The settingsValue function may not handle all valid JSON settings formats, leading to configuration errors
Cross-provider model leakage prevention only clears ai.model for Codex when it contains 'claude', which may not catch all model leakage scenarios
missing: Stress test results using real Claude -p agents to verify provider isolation and boundary enforcement under load
Evidence that tool profile flags (claude_tools, claude_allowed_tools, claude_disallowed_tools) preserve full tool capability under harness boundary
Verification that cross-provider model leakage prevention covers all edge cases beyond Claude model string detection
Testing of boundary enforcement with concurrent project modifications or unusual file system states

result.notes: Provider configuration includes boundary enforcement, provider isolation measures, and tool profile management. Evidence from code, tests, docs, and recent dispatch execution shows the implementation is functioning as designed. Cross-provider model leakage prevention exists but may benefit from additional stress testing with real agents to validate all edge cases.
